payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He has about five or six years before he becomes the voiceover industry. The living embodiment of it. Absorbs us like end of Akira. So that's going to be fun. Look forward to that next week. Let's see, what else?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Bauza is funny as fuck.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He has never played Dungeons and Dragons before.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'm legitimately sad that we're gone next week. Just so we can't be in the wings, to watch it in person.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We'll have a lot of road time to watch it on the way back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: With Bauza at the table, any celebrity could show up. He can impersonate anybody. Anybody.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: High Hierophant Ophera of the Birthheart. There was Earthbreaker Groon. There were Maidens of the Raven. Eventually, Allura and Kima, and J'mon Sa Ord had also come into the fray. During this discussion about what to do, what to handle, you were granted a ring by High Bearer Vord that, within a certain radius, could prevent divinations or specifically scrying on you. Not the School of Divination, but magics that divine your location, whatever's in your vicinity. This moment, the council was interrupted by an image of Vecna taunting you, until it was dispelled by Allura. The discussion was that something was going on in the mountain range where Vecna seemed to have Thar Amphala, which had been discovered by a couple of surviving wyvern riders. Thinking about the two possible locations that could be of interest to Vecna, you gathered your materials, your preparations, and with the first striking lot of wyvern riders, on the back of the newly transformed J'mon Sa Ord into the Devo'ssa dragon form, you were transported via druids to the northern icy landscape of Issylra, to the base of the Zenwick Mountains. You discovered that the Crypts of Thomara, an ancient, forgotten dwarven society that had destroyed itself through cannibalism and madness was now a sinkhole in the mountain range, and a series of large, heavy, 150-plus-foot-across cavernous openings in the ground formed a pattern that you soon discovered were footfalls. Steps. You came upon the risen undead primordial earth titan, long left beneath the surface of the world, dead since the Founding, now given unlife and walking its way southeast towards Vasselheim. Atop its shoulders, you saw Thar Amphala, protected by some sort of magical barrier. There you struggled with a few fleets of flying gloomstalkers that were charging their way towards you. You turned yourself invisible through both Scanlan and J'mon Sa Ord-- J'mon or Devo'ssa: both forms, same person-- you managed to, thanks to a very high roll from Keyleth, discover there was a waterfall or trickle of liquid near the hip of this giant titan. Its giant, four, swinging arms of imminent danger in its vicinity only managed to clip Keyleth and Vax once. Surviving the impact, you all gathered at this ingress right around the hip of the titan, where you leapt off the dragon and proceeded to make your way in. Keyleth, using Control Water, was keeping the heavy flow of an underground river from blowing you off the broken steps that lead into your entry. And that is where we left off. At this time, Keyleth, as you're all standing, looking over your shoulder, you can see the opening where you entered. It's this giant view through this window where you can see down below the mountains and the valleys and the landscapes outside. You can hear the wind blowing though and battering like a heavy, deep whistle. Oh! And we have the rest of our party. Perfect timing. Welcome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, everyone was there.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Early Age of Arcanum, so best estimate from what you knew, you guys both learning of the history of this, well over 1,500 years, if not longer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Investigation, or nature check?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This would not be a nature check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: My better one? That doesn't count. No, nope. Four total.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Much of this tunnel seems to be natural cavern, but there were steps when you first came in. There were stone steps that were carved or placed within the dirt that eventually were consumed or covered by silt and loose earth and whatever the river knocked loose and began to flow over it. So you can see both. A lot of this is natural, but there seem to be, at least in the 30 or 40 feet you've made into the tunnel, you've seen elements of civilization, of crafted entry, that merge with the terrain.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did we get a time frame of how long ago this crypt was built before? How old this crypt has been around?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you guys slowly make your way along the path, behind Vax, you look out and occasionally see bits of loose earth that looks looser than most. As you brush it aside, you can see the steps continue. A lot of it's been buried, but this was a staircase that slowly ascended and curved. The movement of the titan seems to have knocked a lot of the loose tunnel to cover it. As you brush it aside, you carefully look about, and you can see that there are occasional symbols of a writing you don't understand, but nothing stands out to you as a trap or a trigger.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I touch the ground, now that we're in the titan? I'm thinking maybe this might be where the Knuckles are from. Can I touch it and see if the oven mitts do anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Their general, faint vibration never stops. There's a perpetual, very minute hum to them. Touching it doesn't seem to have any effect or change. The proximity seems to be triggering some sort of harmonization.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I reach up and touch my Adam's apple and (vibrating) this place is crazy. Sorry. No, you wouldn't do it? I thought you would.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so make an investigation check, Vax, as you slowly lead the group up this slightly curving tunnel.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is a 27.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 27, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I didn't get to sort them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, you should do that now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Chaos. Introducing chaos.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It takes you about two to three minutes to sound this out, which knocks about two to three minutes off of your concentration time for your Control Water spell. But this looks to be carved in as a warning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does it look familiar to any of us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What languages do you guys know?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Why are you even looking over here? I know stick figures. I know charades.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do any of you know Dwarven?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You can read Dwarven now! You can read now, Grog. You can read four- to five-letter words.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I can read Dwarven because of the belt.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But only tiny bits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I might fuck up the bigger pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Grog, as Vax points out some of these symbols, you sit there and concentrate really hard on one of the carvings in the recently uncovered stone step. As you focus, you begin to sound out the sound to yourself repeatedly. (hesitant syllables) You're trying to find the right way to pronounce it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Baa-ram-ewe. Babe, anyone?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The rough translation from what you can-- spending about five minutes or so breaking out this glyph and a few other glyphs, it says, "Leave. Death here. No further."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Baa-ram-ewe. Babe, anyone?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It takes you about two to three minutes to sound this out, which knocks about two to three minutes off of your concentration time for your Control Water spell. But this looks to be carved in as a warning.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Du. Du hast. Du hast mich. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Strangely, if you could have seen Thomara when it was a highly functioning society, they would have been blasting Rammstein everywhere, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Speak Rammstein and enter.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, these weren't the happy-go-lucky dwarves of old. This was a brutal clan.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It looks like a warning of some kind.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Between all of you guys able to help, it's a negligible period of time, and it wasn't too heavy of an impact. You manage to get them free over the next five or so minutes. They get their breath back. A bit battered, but you get the sense that you should travel quickly.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Dex, huh?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pike needs to do it too, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20. Gil coming in in the clutch!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 13 for the other gnome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Everyone but Grog, you glance up just in time to watch as a large piece of the tunnel ceiling dislodges and comes plummeting down in your direction. Grog, you dive out of the way, being clipped off the shoulder, but you manage to avoid most of the impact. The rest of you, however, immediately get buried by rock and broken earth, suffering 29 points of bludgeoning damage to Percival, Pike, and Scanlan. And they are buried and restrained right now, unable to breathe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I go over and I start-- boulders, dirt, everything.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Picking up the pace, you continue up the curvature of the upward ascension. It's probably been about 60 or 70 feet since you've seen any carved portion of terrain, any sign of previous dwarven construct or building or architecture. As the tunnel stops at the curve, it eventually, looking ahead, ascends a bit, about a ten-foot climb up onto a plateau of some kind. You can see some weird root-like tree is growing out of one wall and seems to be dead and petrified in its current space. Do you guys wish to ascend to the plateau?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I go over and I start-- boulders, dirt, everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Between all of you guys able to help, it's a negligible period of time, and it wasn't too heavy of an impact. You manage to get them free over the next five or so minutes. They get their breath back. A bit battered, but you get the sense that you should travel quickly.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We'll keep going quickly, then.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So as you do that, this entire section of the wall (squishing) turns to a heavy, soft mud material, and your arms plunge into it, and you feel, and you grab Percival, and pull him out. You're covered in thick, sludgy mud, and as he's pulled out, you guys glance around and you can see, shifting in the darkness, the walls in certain places seem to almost move. I need you all to roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We entered in the left side of the body?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you were to look at it straight on, like it's facing you, it would be the right side.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: If it's facing us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So our right, its left.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. Its right, our left.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Looking at its butt.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you're looking at its butt, to the right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right, to the right leg. So if we split, we probably want to keep to the core of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So go left? To the left.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That is a decision, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We'll check, obviously, for air currents.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At which point, there is what sounds like an explosion of rock and dirt immediately to the left of Percival as two heavy, rock-like limbs reach out, grab, and pull him against the wall as the ceiling crumples over and collapses. He has now vanished within the wall, and now leaving a pile of rock in his place.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Percy! Keyleth! Keyleth, dig him out!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Darkness. And you're now restrained, and there is no air.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I start clawing at the wall where he was.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You start clawing it. At this point, with the alertness piqued up between you two and Keyleth, you glance around and you can see things emerging out of the nearby walls. Like the rocks themselves are almost pushing through.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I say, get to the middle, stay away from the walls! And I cast Transmute Rock, right in the space where he went, and I reach my hands in and try and grab him out of mud.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's a 3rd-level spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Have you ever transmuted anything before?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I've done it once before, and it didn't work very well.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Pushing close to an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 16 plus five lightning damage. And then, since he is next to Scanlan, I get sneak attack?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, 12. 15 sneak attack! And then Hunter's Mark, three.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Fantastic. 39 damage in one hit. So the second one, as it hits, it causes this rift in the center of its large, battering ram-like arm to shift and crack and pieces of broken shale peel and fall off to the ground around it. Does that end your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to move, or do you want to stay where you are?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to stay where I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Vax, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We've been in here under an hour. Probably not much time left, but under an hour, right? Since we arrived?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As it slams with both fists into you, with one giant (whoosh) over the head as it impacts you, you grab with one hand the back of its fist and grin, and then bring the axe straight up into its torso with one hand. It strikes and splits open part of its rock chest and you hear this (guttural wheeze) which it reforms again. But still, it's taken the brunt of the impact, and it's now going to shift into the wall, which you can't attack because you already used your reaction. That ends their turn. Keyleth, you're up. You're currently buried. You're blinded.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, not this guy, they're still walking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And the guy who's right there is going to take two strikes at you, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, bring it. They both hit. They're reckless.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep. Yep. And the other attack-- Yep, both hit. So that's 16 points of bludgeoning damage, reduced to eight. And 12 points of bludgeoning damage, reduced to six.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Six. Can I take my retaliation strike against him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Lovely. Reckless, Great Weapon Master. Come on. Natural 20!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's going to suck!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Squish him! Make a building out of him! I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh! I reroll ones and twos! Great. 11, that's better.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You get all sorts of bonuses to that crit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Now I've got David Bowie in my head.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 48 points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, necrotic. 52.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is that doubled? And you doubled everything into your extra crit--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I've been practicing.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, make a strength check. I'll give you advantage because you are a siege monster.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Do I see Trinket underground?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can sense Trinket is currently buried.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, thank you so much!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Jesus, that scared the shit out of me.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You said "I want coffee," and you got coffee?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know! But I didn't think anybody was going to do it, really!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I want a pony!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I want ice cream!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Fly into the air!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I want that PowerBall winnings.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If anyone could do it, Ryan Green could. All righty. So, Keyleth, you can sense Trinket is buried over there, and Pike.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do I go for the bear, or do I go for the gnome?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Go for the gnome!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All right, can I get to Pike in 50 feet?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What's your movement?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 50 feet glide? You can.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Can I very gently grab the gnome, and then siege monster bust us out?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll say I'll give you an action for Trinket instead of an attack to attempt to break free. Make a strength check. Strength saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So with that, as you see it crawling towards Percival, trying to grow and leap at it, and Grog leaps up in its way, there's a very little space where you can actually see its movement. And as you watch Grog pull back with the axe to almost go for a secondary strike, that brief opening, you see you have both arrows notched and releasing both at the same time and as it does it sinks into both of the sockets where the dark void of its eyes were. And it goes (creaking, crashing) and then falls into scattered pieces of rock.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're welcome!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, very nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Do you want to move, or stay where you are?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to stay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Vax, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait! What about Trinket? Can he try to save against it?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, the wings vanish. You can take a short rest if you guys would like to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It could just be a fight to take away our stuff, before we go to a bigger fight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All you find is the door on the left appears to be unlocked and you could possibly push it open, and you see the dwarven writing on the outside of the wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (sighs)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ah, come on. We're running out of time.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What are we fucking pretending like--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can we see, well, can Pike, "Can I see into the hovel?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to push the door open and have a look inside?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it's closed?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The door is closed, you can see--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, hold on, hold on. There are runes, of which I can read.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, five minutes go by, and--?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Don't knock it, all right, just be grateful. Can I try to take a look at the Dwarvish?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You may.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It takes you roughly 15 or so minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, come on! We cannot wait for 15 minutes!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Vax's wings go (poof).

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, the wings vanish. You can take a short rest if you guys would like to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It could just be a fight to take away our stuff, before we go to a bigger fight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All you find is the door on the left appears to be unlocked and you could possibly push it open, and you see the dwarven writing on the outside of the wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (sighs)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ah, come on. We're running out of time.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What are we fucking pretending like--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can we see, well, can Pike, "Can I see into the hovel?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to push the door open and have a look inside?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it's closed?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The door is closed, you can see--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, hold on, hold on. There are runes, of which I can read.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, five minutes go by, and--?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Don't knock it, all right, just be grateful. Can I try to take a look at the Dwarvish?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You may.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It takes you roughly 15 or so minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, come on! We cannot wait for 15 minutes!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Vax's wings go (poof).

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right so you guys can take your hit dice as a short rest. Your form reverts, which if you wanted to try and peek within, you couldn't anyway, because it's worked stone so you can't glide through that. The words you pull out from this are, "The last king, stalwart warrior of our mountain secrets, his might unmatched, his will unbroken, his passing invites the madness."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (sighs)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ah, come on. We're running out of time.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What are we fucking pretending like--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can we see, well, can Pike, "Can I see into the hovel?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to push the door open and have a look inside?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it's closed?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The door is closed, you can see--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, hold on, hold on. There are runes, of which I can read.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, five minutes go by, and--?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Don't knock it, all right, just be grateful. Can I try to take a look at the Dwarvish?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You may.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It takes you roughly 15 or so minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, come on! We cannot wait for 15 minutes!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Vax's wings go (poof).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, the wings vanish. You can take a short rest if you guys would like to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: While he's doing that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm Professor X.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It'll take a while, so it means you lose your invisibility--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, might as well.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Grog, you do not get a short rest unfortunately.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Shit, okay, all right. It's been worth the wait. It is the last king, there's like a secret buried with him and his death brought on the madness. So like, there's definitely treasure with him down there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Unless it's already been raided?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think he's still in there and he's maybe been turning people mad. That would be my guess.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The last king.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: There's a king in there?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What, say it again?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He's the last king, there's like, the secret with--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Stalwart warrior of our mountain secrets."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Mountain dwarf?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Stalwart warrior of our mountain secrets.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: "When I'm turning undead, if they're like little piddly guys, they blow up, but if he's a king he might not blow up."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, then we can--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: "So he might get scared of me, or nothing, I don't know?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Double Pike is the best Pike.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's like there's magic in the world, Liam, that allows you to fly 400 feet a round!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you pull the lid up, (trap triggers) the same toxic fume emanates from it but you are not there in the space, instead appearing right next to the central cylindrical platform in the center of the chamber and look back and the cloud slowly dissipates around. The lid has now fallen back into place.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Aw, shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That was hot.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I walk over to it (finger swirling sound).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, the lid fell back in place.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I did that to the top! I open it up (finger swirling sound).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you open it up. Make a constitution saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As it (cloud explosion) sprays in the air--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It does it again?! It didn't expend?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: These don't!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I would've thought there'd be a little container--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They reset when they close!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: With what?! Are there a bunch of gnomes down there?!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's like a paintball gun.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: On a lockpick DC of 30, you manage to somehow find the intricacy of a king dwarf sarcophagus sealed door. It takes you a good two to three minutes to carefully find the proper tumblers and hold them without it breaking or ruining the lock, but you manage to feel something shift, and a slight latch mechanism on the other side go (creaking). At which point, the brass door from the front door's jaw goes (door opening) and pours forth this green fume that fills the entirety of the chamber. Scanlan, Pike, and Vax, I need you and you because you're still in the back of the chamber--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Look at that face.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you mind if I give a second opinion?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know, what could it hurt? Oh, not that good though. No, lower than what you rolled.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit. There's no traps.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's okay, you're good now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We need a key though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Does appear to be locked, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah, maybe you should pick the lock.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We need a key. Let me try my--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, let's just pick his for this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To?

preceding_turns.text: Pick it.

preceding_turns.text: Pick the lock? Okay.

preceding_turns.text: I'm not in this room.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm in the back, ready.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pike is, though. She's been doing this for five minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you and Pike, you're in the back of the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm right by the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What's everyone doing?

preceding_turns.text: All right. So it's a total of what?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

preceding_turns.text: Wisdom saving throw DC, oh shit, so many pieces of paper. 18.

preceding_turns.text: She casts Turn Undead.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.text: One round?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're not in there.

payload.raw_text: You guys exit the chamber, you're now outside of the cylindrical tomb of the Last King.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Oh no, you can fly over.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: The bronze plate armor is ruined. It's decorative, it appears to be more for visual and presentation than actual function. So you grab the dark hammer and the belt. Then you pull out of the chamber. The jaw of the open bronze is slowly (creaking sound).

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No one knows about this. The sheer force of the first dagger sends it off the broken statue and it falls into the water and it is now unmoving.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nobody that eats with their hands in the inside of a titan is nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Second attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Second attack is 32. Third attack is 27.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gentle old man impression) Thank god you're here! I've been--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There's no throat left.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (rugged voice) Would you like to see my wares--

preceding_turns.text: I've been freed from my--

preceding_turns.text: (gravelly voice) Don't go in the crypt of-- (laughter)

preceding_turns.text: So the first dagger does--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The two daggers hit nothing as the first dagger strikes and slaughters the creature. And hits the water and now floats up, unmoving in the pond there. You can see the liquid around it darkening.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You just killed an NPC!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're not in Emon in the market square!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Well, right now, it's charging, and I need you to roll initiative.

preceding_turns.text: So if dudes came in here and put stuff in sacks, there wouldn't be scrapes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do notice grooves in places and signs that this vault has already been largely raided, of course; most everything in here has been taken. Probably a long time ago, based on the center. You do notice that one of the piles of rags begins to twitch and shift ahead of you.

preceding_turns.text: Mini Jenga.

preceding_turns.text: Mini Jenga?

preceding_turns.text: Well, there's something under that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I've become Death, Destroyer of Worlds.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. This is all you!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Kill it!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Detect undead?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Your last one?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: My last one. Detect undead, Divine Sense.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, Divine Sense. The rag pile, it's around this portion of rock and fallen debris. Behind it, there's that pile of rags that's shifting. The minute you focus, you sense that is indeed a source of undead energy, and at that point, the rags rise up and you see a very long, greenish humanoid claw extend. As the rag falls back, you see a familiar sight. You've encountered trolls in the wild before, but these trolls appear to be somewhat rotting, their flesh strangely battling between decomposition and regeneration. There's this shifting midpoint where its form and whatever cursed enchantment is held within it or disease that claims its body is battling its nature.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How far away is it?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So. It's fine for the last one. Since you took it to the face.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no. This is something weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm drunk. (laughs)

preceding_turns.text: Is it like if you roll below a certain thing you hit yourself instead?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: 23, so half of that is 12. 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you pull back and (whack). You're taken aback by it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, roll high!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Reckless, great weapon master.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why do you always get the fucked-up weapon?

preceding_turns.text: Natural 20!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

preceding_turns.text: Let's do that.

preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

At which point, you watch and you feel the (rumbling), the most recent footfall of the titan. The whole ceiling right there in the corner goes (crashing). This entire chamber.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

preceding_turns.text: I'm going to check that trunk. The chest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's concentration for up to one day, but if you lose concentration halfway through--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Whatever other tunnel options, there's probably other monsters, trolls, fucking undead waiting there, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: This is on you, Grog. No, don't use any spells. Don't use any spells. Grog, punch through this shit.

preceding_turns.text: Wait, I can Commune with Nature and maybe figure out the tunnel system, or depending on where we are and if we think we're on that time crunch and we want to get going-- I didn't want to burn this yet. I did bring Find the Path, which is a one-day concentration spell which gets us to where we want to go as quick as possible. But I don't know. Can I do that with Thar Amphala?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't you need to know where you're going for that spell to work?

That hold that it had on you is lifted.
